name: Jenna Kruse, EMILY's List
subject: When you're not at the table, you're on the menu >>
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7b5e/24c69fd9/d3600c33/18e6cdd5/2768293352/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFE641N_digital_email_20170628_TrumpcarePreview_inact&source=WQEFE641N_digital_email_20170628_TrumpcarePreview_inact")
---

Peter,

The saying "If you're not at the table, you're on the menu" was meant for the Senate Republicans' Trumpcare bill.

Mitch McConnell had 13 men put it together. Not a single woman was included, not even Republican ones who _serve on the health care committee,_ and IT SHOWS.

Between defunding Planned Parenthood, slashing Medicaid, curbing abortion access, and endangering essential health benefits like prenatal care, it's clear that this is the worst bill for women's health in a generation.

While the Senate vote may now be delayed, we need to remain vigilant. **And we need to make it clear that any Republican who supports this blatant attack on women won't be in the Senate for long.

[Donate $3 to elect pro-choice Democratic women who can unseat anti-woman Republicans and help meet our $250,000 End-of-Quarter fundraising goal.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7b5e/24c69fd9/d3600c33/18e6cdd6/2768293352/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170626_email_EOQ?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+to+elect+prochoice+Democratic+w&utm_campaign=WQEFE641N_digital_email_20170628_TrumpcarePreview_inact&source=WQEFE641N_digital_email_20170628_TrumpcarePreview_inact")**

As it's currently written, Trumpcare would put women between a rock and a hard place.

Birth control, no longer covered by insurance, could be prohibitively expensive. With Planned Parenthood defunded, a critical source of access to contraception -- not to mention STI testing and treatment and cancer screenings -- would be in serious danger.

Anyone who receives tax credits to buy insurance would be banned from buying a plan that covers abortion, making it much harder to access and pay for than it already is. (And it's already pretty bad.)

Insurance companies could be allowed to simply not cover pregnancy, maternity, and newborn care -- forcing women and families to pay out of pocket.

And, because that's just not enough, Medicaid would be slashed. Medicaid pays for about half of all births in this country. It keeps moms and babies healthy both during pregnancy and after the baby is born.

The GOP seems to have it out for women like never before, and the cruel impacts of this bill are honestly mind-boggling.

But there IS something we can do: Oust any Republican who supports this heartless legislation and replace them with a pro-choice Democratic woman who will actually protect our care.

**Donate $3 to help take on Trumpcare-supporting Republicans and elect pro-choice Democratic women who will protect Planned Parenthood, Medicaid, and all the programs women and families rely on for health care:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Protect-Our-Health-Care](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7b5e/24c69fd9/d3600c33/18e6cdd7/2768293352/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170626_email_EOQ?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgProtectOurHealth&utm_campaign=WQEFE641N_digital_email_20170628_TrumpcarePreview_inact&source=WQEFE641N_digital_email_20170628_TrumpcarePreview_inact")**

Thanks,

Jenna Kruse
Vice President of Research, EMILY's List

[](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7b5e/24c69fd9/d3600c33/18e6cdd0/2768293352/VEsC/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170626_email_EOQ?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=4&utm_campaign=WQEFE641N_digital_email_20170628_TrumpcarePreview_inact&source=WQEFE641N_digital_email_20170628_TrumpcarePreview_inact")

name: Team Kasich
subject: The latest
body:
Gov. John Kasich was in Washington, D.C. this week to join
Colorado Gov. John Hickenlooper (D) for a press conference
calling on the U.S. Senate to scrap the highly partisan
healthcare reform bill and try again on a piece of legislation
that can win some Democrat votes too

Peter, Gov. John Kasich was in
Washington, D.C. this week to join Colorado Gov. John
Hickenlooper (D) for a press conference calling on the U.S.
Senate to scrap the highly partisan healthcare reform bill and
try again on a piece of legislation that can win some Democrat
votes too.

Why?

The lesson that should have been
learned from Obamacare: something as big as a plan for how the
United States of America addresses not only healthcare, but
Medicaid, the opioid crisis, drug prevention programs and other
programs aimed to help the mentally ill, those with chronic
illness and the working poor, simply cannot offer meaningful and
lasting reforms with only one party involved.

Watch the event here:

Governors Kasich and Hickenlooper
were on CNN's "The Lead with Jake Tapper"
Part 1

Part 2

Read More: New York Times: How
Governors From Both Parties Plotted to Derail the Senate Health
Bill

"At the center of the effort has
been a pair of low-key moderates: Gov. John R. Kasich, Republican
of Ohio, and Gov. John W. Hickenlooper, Democrat of Colorado, who
on Tuesday morning called on the Senate to reject the Republican
bill and to negotiate a bipartisan alternative.

Just before Senate Republicans
delayed a vote on the bill, Mr. Kasich denounced his own party's
legislation in biting terms, saying it would victimize the poor
and mentally ill, and redirect tax money "to people who are
already very wealthy."

"This bill," Mr. Kasich said, "is
unacceptable."

The mounting criticism from
governors, including sharp denunciations from within President
Trump's party, helped stymie Republican efforts to marshal
support in the Senate, and may have led, in a roundabout way, to
the stalling of the measure this week…"

"But if the legislation does
unravel further, governors will have played a hand: Mr.
Hickenlooper said in an interview that he and Mr. Kasich had
agreed to team up after a February meeting of the governors'
association in Washington, where state leaders heard an alarming
presentation about the potential consequences of a federal
pullback in health care.

Within weeks, Mr. Hickenlooper
said, both Mr. Kasich and Mr. Sandoval had sought his help in
taking on their own party. Mr. Kasich, the Colorado governor
recalled, expressed confidence that he could find other
Republicans who would "take a pretty strong stand that coverage
shouldn't be rolled back."

A tentative game plan emerged:
They would assemble a nimble, informal group of governors, from
the right and left of center, who would publicly express concern
about health care legislation drafted in the House and Senate.
The governors would press for a slower, less disruptive and more
public legislative process, and insist on protections for states
that had greatly expanded their Medicaid rolls.

Joining Mr. Kasich and Mr.
Sandoval on the Republican side was Mr. Baker. On the Democratic
side, Mr. Hickenlooper recruited Steve Bullock of Montana, John
Bel Edwards of Louisiana and Tom Wolf of Pennsylvania." Read the
full article (here).

Want to help? Please take a
moment to follow Gov. Kasich on Facebook and Twitter to see the
latest updates:

Facebook.com/JohnKasich
--
Twitter.com/JohnKasich

-Team Kasich

for America, not authorized by any candidate or candidate's
committee.

body:
1600 Daily Everything White House for 6/29/2017. |

After seven failed years of Obamacare, it is time to put the American people back in control of their own health care. President Donald J. Trump and his Administration continue to work with Congress to repeal and replace the disaster that is Obamacare.

2:00 PM: Press Briefing with Principal Deputy Press Secretary Sarah Sanders and Secretary of the Treasury Steven Mnuchin -

body:
Let's close June strong. **Before our end-of-quarter deadline tomorrow, pitch in $3 or more to help elect Democrats all across the country.
**[DONATE](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405233d9/4067b0a/364ba69bb/7e6b91d4/826172555/VEsH/ "https://my.democrats.org/June-Deadline-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20170629_af_nd&source=em_20170629_af_nd&refcode=em_20170629_af_nd")****
_We have a huge fundraising deadline coming up in less than 36 hours, so if you don't have time to read this email right now, please**[click here and help us meet our fundraising goal!](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405233d9/4067b0a/364ba69bb/7e6b91d5/826172555/VEsE/ "https://my.democrats.org/June-Deadline-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+click+here+and+help+us+meet+our+fundrais&utm_campaign=em_20170629_af_nd&source=em_20170629_af_nd&refcode=em_20170629_af_nd")**_

\-- Al

Dear Derek,

There's no wrong way to stand up to Donald Trump and stand up for our shared values -- as long as you do as much of it as you can, as often as you can. It's gotta be part of your everyday routine: **eat, sleep, resist, repeat.**

That said, I know you already have a million _other_ things you're supposed to do every day: exercise, moisturize, eat more leafy greens.

**[So here's a way to fight against Trump that will take you less than 60 seconds: click here and make a contribution to help elect Democrats!](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405233d9/4067b0a/364ba69bb/7e6b91d2/826172555/VEsF/ "https://my.democrats.org/June-Deadline-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+So+heres+a+way+to+fight+against+Trump+th&utm_campaign=em_20170629_af_nd&source=em_20170629_af_nd&refcode=em_20170629_af_nd")**

Democrats across the country are organizing every hour of every day to stop Trump and defeat the Republicans enabling him. Contributions from folks like you -- even donations of as little as $3 -- are what keep our grassroots machine running, day in and day out.

**But today is especially critical, because we've got a huge fundraising deadline staring us right in the face -- it's less than 36 hours away!**

So while we need you to stand with us _every_ day, we _really_ need you **today**. As in **right now.**

I know you're busy, but it won't take long: please click here to make a contribution right now.

**If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately.

[ QUICK DONATE: $3](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405233d9/4067b0a/364ba69bb/7e6b91d3/826172555/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/june-deadline-2017?express_lane=true&amount=3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=4+-+QUICK+DONATE+3&utm_campaign=em_20170629_af_nd&source=em_20170629_af_nd&refcode=em_20170629_af_nd")
[QUICK DONATE: $10](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405233d9/4067b0a/364ba69bb/7e6b91d0/826172555/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/june-deadline-2017?express_lane=true&amount=10&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=5+-+QUICK+DONATE+10&utm_campaign=em_20170629_af_nd&source=em_20170629_af_nd&refcode=em_20170629_af_nd")
[QUICK DONATE: $25](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405233d9/4067b0a/364ba69bb/7e6b91d1/826172555/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/june-deadline-2017?express_lane=true&amount=25&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=6+-+QUICK+DONATE+25&utm_campaign=em_20170629_af_nd&source=em_20170629_af_nd&refcode=em_20170629_af_nd")
[QUICK DONATE: $50](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405233d9/4067b0a/364ba69bb/7e6b91de/826172555/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/june-deadline-2017?express_lane=true&amount=50&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=7+-+QUICK+DONATE+50&utm_campaign=em_20170629_af_nd&source=em_20170629_af_nd&refcode=em_20170629_af_nd")
[QUICK DONATE: $100](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405233d9/4067b0a/364ba69bb/7e6b91df/826172555/VEsO/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/june-deadline-2017?express_lane=true&amount=100&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=8+-+QUICK+DONATE+100&utm_campaign=em_20170629_af_nd&source=em_20170629_af_nd&refcode=em_20170629_af_nd")
[Or donate another amount.](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405233d9/4067b0a/364ba69bb/7e6b91dc/826172555/VEsP/ "https://my.democrats.org/June-Deadline-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=9+-+Or+donate+another+amount&utm_campaign=em_20170629_af_nd&source=em_20170629_af_nd&refcode=em_20170629_af_nd")
** Thanks -- and don't forget to moisturize.

Al

P.S. -- This deadline is coming up quick -- less than 36 hours to go. Frankly, I was hoping you'd have clicked on a link and made a contribution already. **[But there's still time: click here to give!](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405233d9/4067b0a/364ba69bb/7e6b91dd/826172555/VEsHBQ/ "https://my.democrats.org/June-Deadline-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=10+-+But+theres+still+time+click+here+to+give&utm_campaign=em_20170629_af_nd&source=em_20170629_af_nd&refcode=em_20170629_af_nd")**

I'll keep this short.

name: Al Franken
subject: this garbage health care bill
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
Mitch McConnell just announced that he's postponing a vote on the GOP bill to repeal Obamacare. Now, keep up the fight.
---
**[](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/protect-our-care?refcode=DPE-20170629-Franken-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")**
Dear Team,

You know how you've been making calls and writing postcards and showing up at town halls to protest Trumpcare? Well, it's working. Republicans are so nervous about their garbage health care bill, they've put off the vote.

For now.

**But here 's what Mitch McConnell is telling them:**_Relax. It 'll blow over. The President will tweet something insane, and everyone will get distracted, and we can bring this bill back. Heck, by the next election, everyone will have forgotten how we took away tens of millions of people's health care without so much as a public hearing._

Well, not in exactly those words. But that's what he's thinking.

**And it 's up to us to show every single Republican that we're not letting up.**

[**Click here right now to donate to the DSCC and show Republicans that we 're never going to stop fighting!**](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/protect-our-care?refcode=DPE-20170629-Franken-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

Ordinarily, I'm not much for punditry. I'd rather talk about the issues. And that's exactly what I've done since the moment this bill was created in the shadows -- showing how it would jack up costs for seniors, force rural hospitals to close, and ruin the lives of millions of American families.

But the math is what it is. There are 52 Republicans in the Senate. If just a few more of them start to think it's safe to support this bill, it's going to pass, and people are going to get hurt.

And while we can continue to try to reason with them, it may be that the only way to stop Republicans from moving forward with this bill is to scare them out of it.

**In other words: We need them to understand that, if they ever let this bill come back, it 'll cost them their jobs in the next election.**

[**And the best way to do that is to put our money where our values are -- and donate to the DSCC to help us run the most aggressive campaign in our history. Click here to give now!**](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/protect-our-care?refcode=DPE-20170629-Franken-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

I don't know what's going to happen from here on out. But I'm going to keep fighting like hell to protect people's health care. And I need your help -- not just to make the case for why it's so important, but to make my Republican colleagues understand that we won't let them sneak this bill through. Not now, not ever.

Thanks for doing your part.

Al

P.S.: We need to show Republicans we're serious, and we need to do it **now** \-- before Mitch McConnell can convince them to change their minds and put this bill on the floor for a vote. [**Please click here to make a donation and help me show my Republican colleagues that we aren 't going to let this go -- there's just too much at stake.**](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/protect-our-care?refcode=DPE-20170629-Franken-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

120 Maryland Ave. NE
Washington, DC 20002** Paid for by the DSCC, dscc.org,

If you'd like to make a contribution to support us, please [click here](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e4cf/2b07114c/24f735e6/73107294/1999216048/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/supportdscc?refcode=DPE-disclaimer"). Together, we're going to take back the Senate, but we'll need the help of every single grassroots supporter to do it. If you'd like to stop receiving all emails from the DSCC, please [click here](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e4cf/2b07114c/24f735e6/73107297/1999216048/VEsB/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/content/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com"). body:
| This fight is about our fundamental values. And, Democrats are not going to back down.

Did you see this from Senator Van Hollen yesterday? _He can count on me; can he count on you?_

[**http://action.vanhollen.org/CVH-FEC_2017**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bc4/70cdc2ba/47711d88/397c45e/376334952/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cvh-june17?refcode=em062917_1&amount=25.00&recurring=12&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+httpactionvanhollenorgCVHFEC2017&utm_campaign=em062817&source=em062817")

Begin forwarded message:

> As the great American philosopher Yogi Berra said, "It ain't over till it's over." It's good news that the Senate Republicans don't have the votes this week to pass their unconscionable Wealthcare bill. But this is no time for any kind of victory lap.
>
> Over the next few weeks the Senate Republicans will redouble their efforts to try to repeal Obamacare after the July 4th break. You can be sure that Republican Senate Leader Mitch McConnell and President Trump will try every trick in the book to get the votes they need. That means we have to fight back even harder.
>
> [**Add your name if you will join me in reaching out to friends and relatives around the country to urge them to keep the pressure on and demand their senators withhold support for this reckless bill.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bc4/70cdc2ba/47711d88/397c45f/376334952/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/signcvh?refcode=em062817&amount=25.00&recurring=12&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+Add+your+name+if+you+will+join+me+in+rea&utm_campaign=em062817&source=em062817")
>
> I agreed to lead the Democratic Senate Campaign Committee (DSCC) because I believe in our mission of supporting leaders who want to take America forward, not take a U-turn on the progress we have made. Every Democratic senator is opposed to this disastrous GOP bill. I need your help to make sure that the 25 Democratic senators who are up for election in 2018 have all the support and resources it takes to win.
>
> Make no mistake: We need to keep up the fight everyday and everywhere. That's why I need your help right now.
>
> **Friday is our FIRST Federal Election Commission (FEC) filing deadline since the Senate Republicans unveiled their immoral bill. Let's show that we will pull out all the stops to block this bill. And let's show that we will support our class of 2018 which is holding the Blue Line in the U.S. Senate. Our goal is to raise another $45,000 to send a clear message that we are standing firm. And that means starting right now.
>
> [Contribute now to help turn Congress blue.](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bc4/70cdc2ba/47711d88/397c45c/376334952/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cvh-june17?refcode=em062917_1&amount=25.00&recurring=12&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=3+-+Contribute+now+to+help+turn+Congress+blu&utm_campaign=em062817&source=em062817")**

> _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately by clicking a button below. Otherwise, you will be redirected to a donation page._

>

>
> ******[Other amount](http://action.vanhollen. But, Democrats' route to victory will be built on the enthusiasm, energy, and engagement of our grassroots power.

> Chris

name: Russ Feingold, LegitAction
subject: Our path away from frustration
body:
Unless we address the issues of legitimacy in our democracy, we're going to keep being frustrated.
Unless we address the issues of legitimacy in our democracy, we're going to keep being frustrated. | [](http://welcome.legitaction.com/page/m/66cf46e3/1c469400/38d27d7/354bd9f/667432369/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/legitaction_donate?refcode=20170628_EOQ2_msg_1&utm_medium=email&utm_source=feingold&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=20170628_EOQ2_msg_1&source=20170628_EOQ2_msg_1") Friend, Several of the Democratic candidates in this spring's surprisingly close special elections were champions of legitimacy. It can be especially frustrating to see them fall just short. But here's the thing: **unless we successfully address the issues of legitimacy in our democracy, we 're going to keep being frustrated by the outcomes of the important races next year -- and beyond.** It's time for progressives to think big and fight hard for our democracy. It's time to push on issues like the assault on voting rights and hyper-partisan gerrymandering. | **PRESS RELEASE** **For Immediate Release:** June 29, 2017 **Contact:** Patrick Hynes, [202-321-5310](tel:\(202\)%20321-5310)
**Statement from NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester on Kevin Cavanaugh’s support for making Manchester a Sanctuary City**
Today, the New Hampshire Republican Party is calling on Kevin Cavanaugh to clarify his proposal to impose sanctuary city status on the city of Manchester.
Last night at the League of Women Voters Candidate forum, Manchester Alderman and State Senate candidate Kevin Cavanaugh reiterated his support for making Manchester a sanctuary city. When asked by a Bow voter if he believed that Manchester should become a sanctuary city, Cavanaugh responded, “I think we should accept people. Yes I think we should...that’s a great process to bring people into this country and to make them American Citizens.”
NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester released the following statement regarding Cavanaugh’s comments:
“Imposing sanctuary city status on Manchester or any other city or town in New Hampshire is both foolish and dangerous. Sanctuary cities from Baltimore to Chicago have seen consistently higher rates of violent crime than non-sanctuary cities. This is on top of the potential loss of federal funding that will almost certainly accompany the designation. Cavanaugh’s proposal risks both the safety and well-being of every man, woman and child in Manchester and throughout the state.”
“We simply cannot put our children at risk for Kevin Cavanaugh’s short-term political gain.”
### |

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: A lesson from the Maasai tribe
body:
#
**[Everyone wants better opportunities for their kids. Help us make that possible by contributing to our campaign to rebuild Florida into a state that works for all of us>>](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb32e0/9e00690/4d74c206/2403653a/3558864335/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-june2017?refcode=em20170629-maasi-rs")**
Team --

If you're familiar with the Maasai Tribe of East Africa, you may know that they greet each other a little differently than we do in the United States. While we might ask, "How are you doing" or "What's up," they ask "How are the children?"

They strive to answer that the children are doing well. And I think that's what we should be striving for here in Florida, too. All of us want to give our kids better opportunities to grow and thrive in our communities.

I'm the father to three incredible children, Caroline and Jackson, who just turned three years old, and Davis, who is one month old. And I'm grateful that R. Jai and I are able to provide them with opportunities that I never dreamed of having growing up. I'm thankful that when I'm asked how my children are doing, I can honestly say they're doing well.

But that's not always the case in this state. If we were to look ourselves in the mirror today, we would have to admit that, as a whole, the children of Florida are not well. Just recently, the Annie E. Casey Foundation ranked Florida 40th in the nation in terms of overall child well-being.

The children of Florida are not well.

They are not well because more than a quarter of them are growing up in poverty.

They are not well because our state has shifted resources from our public schools to private corporations.

They are not well because too many of their families cannot afford healthcare.

They are not well because too many of them cannot afford to go to college without being saddled with a lifetime of debt.

They are not well because their parents are trapped in an economy that delivers record profits for special interests and massive bonuses for those at the top -- and jobs that have shrinking paychecks and too few benefits.

Many of you have reached out to ask about my goals on this campaign, and for the state of Florida. And this is my answer: My goal is to be able to honestly answer that question - "How are the children?" \- with "They are doing well." \- in every county and every corner of this state.

I want to reinvest in our public schools, and ensure that all students have the opportunity to attend college without acquiring a lifetime of debt. I want to expand access to affordable healthcare across our state. I want to invest in creating jobs that pay a living wage, so that all Floridians can have work with dignity. I want to end the vicious cycles of poverty that plague far too many families in our state. **I want to ensure that our children are well.**

[**If you agree, chip in $3 or whatever you can afford to help us fight for Florida 's children in the Governor's office >>**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb32e0/9e00690/4d74c206/2403653a/3558864335/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-june2017?refcode=em20170629-maasi-rs")

For too long, our government has focused on increasing profit margins for a few big corporations, instead of increasing opportunities for all of our children. But with your help, we're going to change that. With your help, we will ensure that our children are doing well.
Thank you for being a part of this movement.

Bring it home,

Andrew
**[CONTRIBUTE](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb32e0/9e00690/4d74c206/2403653a/3558864335/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-june2017?refcode=em20170629-maasi-rs")**

Paid by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can let us know if you'd like to [receive fewer emails](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb32e0/9e00690/4d74c206/2403653d/3558864335/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiJ9/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip="). We'd hate to see you go, but if you need to do so, click here to [unsubscribe](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb32e0/9e00690/4d74c206/2403653c/3558864335/VEsD/ "https://gillum.cp.bsd.net/page/unsubscribe/"). [You can support our campaign by making a donation.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb32e0/9e00690/4d74c206/2403653f/3558864335/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ag-footer")

name: Nina Turner, Our Revolution
subject: A brand new chapter for Our Revolution
body:
I have agreed to become the next President.
#
Friends,
I am extremely humbled to announce a new chapter for Our Revolution. Following a vote by the Board of Directors, I have agreed to become the next President.
Please watch a personal message from me about the next steps in our journey together.
[](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0dcb/43f17890/c6c6f056/62035b6b/2597688389/VEsH/ "https://ourrevolution.com/message-new-president-nina-turner/?source=em170629-full")
From being a board member to now fulfilling the role of president of an organization with you at our heart, I could not be more excited. There is no doubt that we have the will of the people on our side. Let us lift each other up and achieve what we've all been fighting for--ending corporate greed, expanding health care coverage, and building an America that is good and just for everybody.
In solidarity,
Nina Turner
President
Our Revolution

**_Here 's the good news: Chris will never give up._**

He knows the only way to end toxic politics in the Trump era is for Democrats to take back Congress in 2018.

**Will you stand with Chris to help hold Republicans accountable? Tomorrow night is our FIRST FEC filing deadline since the Republicans moved forward with their reckless TrumpCare agenda.

[We must raise another $45,000 to send a clear message that we are organizing to win. name: Al Franken
subject: Contemptible, despicable, execrable
body:
Let's close June strong. **Before our end-of-quarter deadline tomorrow, pitch in $3 or more to help elect Democrats all over the country.
**[DONATE](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405233e3/4067b0a/362f4fd93/7e6a43a6/1514758850/VEsH/ "https://my.democrats.org/June-Deadline-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20170629_af2_nd&source=em_20170629_af2_nd&refcode=em_20170629_af2_nd")****
Dear Derek,

Here's a little tip from a guy who used to be a writer: there's no shame in reaching for a thesaurus when you need to find just the right word.

So, for example, let's say you're talking about Trumpcare. You don't have to settle for calling it "bad." You could call it -- let's see here -- "contemptible," or "despicable," or "execrable." Instead of calling it "cruel," you could jazz it up with a word like "heartless" or "malevolent."

Or let's say you wanted to explain to your friend how important it is that we not let up the fight, even though Republicans aren't going to vote on the bill this week. You could use "critical," or "imperative," or even "vital."

**Because it is.** Sure, we should celebrate the strength we've showed this week, but backing down from the fight now would be a huge mistake. Or, if you prefer, "blunder." Or "goof."

**The truth is, you can use whatever words you want -- because when it comes to fighting the far right, it's not what we _say_ that matters. It's what we _do_.**

When it comes to defeating the Trump agenda and putting our country back on track, all the fifty-cent words in the thesaurus don't mean nearly as much as a $3 (or whatever you can afford) contribution to help elect Democrats across the country.

Click here to donate, contribute, endow, pitch in -- whatever word works for you. The important thing is that you take action today to help us stop the far right!

Or let's say you wanted to explain to your friend how important it is that we not let up the fight, even though Republicans aren't going to vote on the bill this week. Peter,

We are making solid progress on our End-of-Quarter goal.

We've already raised $177,521 -- that's over two-thirds of our $250,000 goal!

The finish line is in sight, and I know we'll get there if everyone chips in a little bit.

**[Step up and donate $3 before midnight tomorrow to hit our goal before June is over.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7b40/24c69fd9/d215e8c1/18e6f24c/308789005/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170626_email_EOQ?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+if+consinConsGroup10583According+to+our+&utm_campaign=WQEFE643N_digital_email_20170629_FRupdate_inact&source=WQEFE643N_digital_email_20170629_FRupdate_inact")**

I'm so proud to see the surge in support from this community at the end of every month.

The goals we set aren't easy, but it's critical that we hit them if we're going to do things like support all nine of our Senate women up for re-election and train _thousands_ of women to run for office for the first time.

**Let's get the rest of the way there before midnight tomorrow -- I know we can do it. Donate $3 to help reach our $250,000 goal:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Reach-Our-Goal](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7b40/24c69fd9/d215e8c1/18e6f24d/308789005/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170626_email_EOQ?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgReachOurGoal&utm_campaign=WQEFE643N_digital_email_20170629_FRupdate_inact&source=WQEFE643N_digital_email_20170629_FRupdate_inact")**

Leigh K. Warren
